library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(forecast)
library(tseries)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
## 
## Attaching package: 'scales'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     discard
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     col_factor
library(nortest)
library(broom)
INPUT_FILE    <- "../../data/processed/merged_features_weekly.csv"
ARIMA_METRICS <- "../arima/outputs/arima_metrics.csv"
OUTPUT_DIR    <- "outputs"
FREQ          <- 52
ALPHA         <- 0.05

XREG_VARS <- c(
  "api_energy_and_lubricants",
  "api_fertilisers_and_soil_improvers",
  "api_plant_protection_products",
  "api_fresh_fruit",
  "api_fresh_vegetables",
  "fuel_petrol_price",
  "fuel_diesel_price",
  "sppi_road_freight"
)

DUMMY_VARS <- c("shock_2021q4_2023q1", "post_shock")
df <- read_csv(INPUT_FILE, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  arrange(date)

# ── FILTER TO TARGET COMMODITIES ─────────────────────────────
target_commodities <- c(
  "Beetroot",
  "bulb onion yellow",
  "cabbage",
  "carrot",
  "cucumber",
  "curly kale",
  "lettuce",
  "tomato round"
)

df <- df %>%
  filter(commodity %in% target_commodities)

commodities <- unique(df$commodity)
cat("Filtered commodities:", paste(commodities, collapse = ", "), "\n")
## Filtered commodities: Beetroot
# ── LOAD PRE-RUN ARIMA BASELINES ──────────────────────────────
arima_baseline <- read_csv(ARIMA_METRICS, show_col_types = FALSE)
print(arima_baseline %>% select(model, RMSE, MAE, MASE, sMAPE, MAPE, AIC, BIC))
## # A tibble: 3 × 8
##   model                              RMSE    MAE  MASE sMAPE  MAPE    AIC    BIC
##   <chr>                             <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 Beetroot — auto.arima            0.0744 0.0529  2.80  7.20  6.77 -1853. -1829.
## 2 Beetroot — ARIMA(1,1,1)          0.0804 0.0601  3.18  8.26  7.74 -1845. -1833.
## 3 Beetroot — SARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)… 0.0737 0.0505  2.68  6.85  6.48 -1514. -1494.
calc_rmse  <- function(actual, pred) sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
calc_mae   <- function(actual, pred) mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE)
calc_mape  <- function(actual, pred) mean(abs((actual - pred) / actual) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
calc_smape <- function(actual, pred) {
  mean(200 * abs(actual - pred) / (abs(actual) + abs(pred)), na.rm = TRUE)
}
calc_mase  <- function(actual, pred, train) {
  naive_mae <- mean(abs(diff(train)), na.rm = TRUE)
  mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE) / naive_mae
}

compile_metrics <- function(actual, pred, train, model_obj, label) {
  tibble(
    model = label,
    RMSE  = round(calc_rmse(actual, pred),        4),
    MAE   = round(calc_mae(actual, pred),         4),
    MASE  = round(calc_mase(actual, pred, train), 4),
    sMAPE = round(calc_smape(actual, pred),       4),
    MAPE  = round(calc_mape(actual, pred),        4),
    AIC   = round(AIC(model_obj),                 2),
    BIC   = round(BIC(model_obj),                 2)
  )
}

residual_diagnostics <- function(model_obj, label) {
  resid_clean <- as.numeric(residuals(model_obj))
  resid_clean <- resid_clean[!is.na(resid_clean)]
  lb10 <- Box.test(resid_clean, lag = 10, type = "Ljung-Box")
  lb20 <- Box.test(resid_clean, lag = 20, type = "Ljung-Box")
  norm_test <- if (length(resid_clean) <= 5000) {
    list(result = shapiro.test(resid_clean), name = "Shapiro-Wilk")
  } else {
    list(result = nortest::ad.test(resid_clean), name = "Anderson-Darling")
  }
  tibble(
    model             = label,
    resid_mean        = round(mean(resid_clean),           6),
    resid_sd          = round(sd(resid_clean),             4),
    ljung_box_lag10_p = round(lb10$p.value,                4),
    ljung_box_lag20_p = round(lb20$p.value,                4),
    normality_test    = norm_test$name,
    normality_p       = round(norm_test$result$p.value,    4),
    autocorr_ok       = lb10$p.value > ALPHA,
    normality_ok      = norm_test$result$p.value > ALPHA
  )
}

plot_residuals <- function(model_obj, label) {
  resid_vec   <- as.numeric(residuals(model_obj))
  fitted_vec  <- as.numeric(fitted(model_obj))
  resid_clean <- resid_vec[!is.na(resid_vec)]
  time_idx    <- seq_along(resid_vec)
  ci_bound    <- qnorm(0.975) / sqrt(length(resid_clean))

  p1 <- ggplot(data.frame(t = time_idx, r = resid_vec), aes(t, r)) +
    geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 0.45) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = "Residuals over time", x = "Week index", y = "Residual") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  acf_data <- acf(resid_clean, plot = FALSE, lag.max = 52)
  acf_df   <- data.frame(lag = acf_data$lag[-1], acf = acf_data$acf[-1])
  p2 <- ggplot(acf_df, aes(lag, acf)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), color = "#2980b9") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-ci_bound, ci_bound), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "ACF of residuals", x = "Lag (weeks)", y = "ACF") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  pacf_data <- pacf(resid_clean, plot = FALSE, lag.max = 52)
  pacf_df   <- data.frame(lag = pacf_data$lag, pacf = pacf_data$acf)
  p3 <- ggplot(pacf_df, aes(lag, pacf)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), color = "#8e44ad") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-ci_bound, ci_bound), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "PACF of residuals", x = "Lag (weeks)", y = "PACF") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  p4 <- ggplot(data.frame(r = resid_clean), aes(r)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40,
                   fill = "#3498db", color = "white", alpha = 0.7) +
    stat_function(fun = dnorm,
                  args = list(mean = mean(resid_clean), sd = sd(resid_clean)),
                  color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = "Residual distribution", x = "Residual", y = "Density") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  qq    <- qqnorm(resid_clean, plot.it = FALSE)
  qq_df <- data.frame(theoretical = qq$x, sample = qq$y)
  p5 <- ggplot(qq_df, aes(theoretical, sample)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "#2c3e50", size = 0.8) +
    geom_abline(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = "Q-Q plot", x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Sample quantiles") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  p6 <- ggplot(data.frame(f = fitted_vec, r = resid_vec), aes(f, r)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 0.9, color = "#16a085") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
                color = "#e67e22", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted", x = "Fitted values", y = "Residual") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  combined <- (p1 | p4) / (p2 | p3) / (p5 | p6) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Residual Diagnostics — ", label),
      subtitle = "Row 1: Time series & distribution | Row 2: ACF & PACF | Row 3: Q-Q & Residuals vs Fitted",
      theme    = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))
    )
  print(combined)
}

plot_coefficients <- function(coef_df, label) {
  reg_coefs <- coef_df %>%
    filter(!str_detect(term, "^ar|^ma|^sar|^sma|intercept|drift")) %>%
    mutate(significant = p_value < ALPHA, term = fct_reorder(term, estimate))

  if (nrow(reg_coefs) == 0) {
    cat("  No regression coefficients to plot for:", label, "\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  p <- ggplot(reg_coefs, aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = significant)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf_lo, xmax = conf_hi), height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("TRUE" = "#27ae60", "FALSE" = "#e74c3c"),
      labels = c("TRUE" = paste0("p < ", ALPHA), "FALSE" = paste0("p >= ", ALPHA)),
      name   = "Significance"
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Regression Coefficients — ", label),
      subtitle = "Error bars = 95% CI | Green = statistically significant",
      x = "Coefficient estimate", y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")
  print(p)
}

plot_forecast <- function(train_dates, train_vals,
                          test_dates,  test_vals,
                          fc_list, label) {
  train_df <- data.frame(date = train_dates, value = train_vals) %>%
    filter(date >= max(date) - 365 * 2)
  test_df  <- data.frame(date = test_dates, value = test_vals)

  model_colors <- c(
    "Train"        = "#bdc3c7",
    "Actual"       = "#2c3e50",
    "Full xreg"    = "#e74c3c",
    "Dummies only" = "#2980b9"
  )

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = train_df, aes(x = date, y = value, color = "Train"), linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_line(data = test_df,  aes(x = date, y = value, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1)

  ribbon_fills <- c("#e74c3c", "#2980b9")
  for (i in seq_along(fc_list)) {
    nm  <- names(fc_list)[i]
    fc  <- fc_list[[i]]
    pdf <- data.frame(
      date = test_dates,
      pred = as.numeric(fc$mean),
      lo95 = as.numeric(fc$lower[, 2]),
      hi95 = as.numeric(fc$upper[, 2])
    )
    p <- p +
      geom_ribbon(data = pdf, aes(x = date, ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95),
                  fill = ribbon_fills[i], alpha = 0.10) +
      geom_line(data = pdf, aes(x = date, y = pred, color = nm),
                linewidth = 0.85, linetype = "dashed")
  }

  p <- p +
    scale_color_manual(values = model_colors, name = NULL) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Regression with ARIMA Errors — Forecast: ", label),
      subtitle = "Dashed = forecast | Shaded = 95% PI | ARIMA baselines in arima_metrics.csv",
      x = NULL, y = "Price"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")
  print(p)
}

extract_coefs <- function(model_obj, label) {
  coef_vals <- coef(model_obj)
  se_vals   <- sqrt(diag(vcov(model_obj)))[names(coef_vals)]
  t_vals    <- coef_vals / se_vals
  p_vals    <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(t_vals))
  tibble(
    model       = label,
    term        = names(coef_vals),
    estimate    = round(coef_vals,                  6),
    std_err     = round(se_vals,                    6),
    t_stat      = round(t_vals,                     4),
    p_value     = round(p_vals,                     4),
    conf_lo     = round(coef_vals - 1.96 * se_vals, 6),
    conf_hi     = round(coef_vals + 1.96 * se_vals, 6),
    significant = p_vals < ALPHA
  )
}

build_xreg <- function(data, vars) {
  avail <- vars[vars %in% names(data)]
  if (length(avail) == 0) return(NULL)
  mat <- data %>% select(all_of(avail)) %>% as.matrix()
  for (j in seq_len(ncol(mat))) {
    col_mean <- mean(mat[, j], na.rm = TRUE)
    mat[is.na(mat[, j]), j] <- col_mean
  }
  mat
}
all_metrics <- list()
all_diag    <- list()
all_coefs   <- list()
for (comm in commodities) {
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  cat("  Commodity:", comm, "\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

  sub <- df %>% filter(commodity == comm) %>% arrange(date)

  if (!"split" %in% names(sub)) stop("'split' column not found in data.")

  train_df <- sub %>% filter(split == "train")
  test_df  <- sub %>% filter(split == "test")

  if (nrow(train_df) < 30 || nrow(test_df) == 0) {
    cat("  !! Insufficient rows — skipping.\n"); next
  }

  n_test    <- nrow(test_df)
  train_ts  <- ts(train_df$target_price, frequency = FREQ)
  safe_name <- str_replace_all(comm, "[ /]", "_")

  cat(sprintf("  Train: %s -> %s (%d weeks)\n",
              min(train_df$date), max(train_df$date), nrow(train_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  Test:  %s -> %s (%d weeks)\n",
              min(test_df$date),  max(test_df$date),  n_test))

  rds_path <- file.path("../arima/outputs/", paste0("arima_auto_", safe_name, ".rds"))
  if (!file.exists(rds_path)) {
    stop(sprintf("RDS not found: %s\n  Run arima_model.R first.", rds_path))
  }
  saved_model <- readRDS(rds_path)
  arma        <- saved_model$arma
  pdq         <- c(arma[1], arma[6], arma[2])
  PDQ         <- c(arma[3], arma[7], arma[4])

  cat(sprintf("  Loaded ARIMA order: ARIMA(%d,%d,%d)(%d,%d,%d)[%d]\n",
              pdq[1], pdq[2], pdq[3], PDQ[1], PDQ[2], PDQ[3], FREQ))

  avail_xreg    <- XREG_VARS[XREG_VARS %in% names(sub) &
                               sapply(XREG_VARS, function(v) sum(!is.na(train_df[[v]])) > 10)]
  avail_dummies <- DUMMY_VARS[DUMMY_VARS %in% names(sub)]

  cat("  xreg vars:", paste(avail_xreg,    collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("  dummies:  ", paste(avail_dummies, collapse = ", "), "\n")

  fc_list <- list()

  # ── Model A: Full xreg ────────────────────
  all_reg_vars    <- c(avail_xreg, avail_dummies)
  xreg_train_full <- build_xreg(train_df, all_reg_vars)
  xreg_test_full  <- build_xreg(test_df,  all_reg_vars)

  cat("\n  Fitting Model A: Full xreg (", ncol(xreg_train_full), "regressors)...\n")
  model_full <- tryCatch(
    Arima(train_ts, order = pdq,
          seasonal = list(order = PDQ, period = FREQ),
          xreg = xreg_train_full, include.drift = FALSE),
    error = function(e) { cat("  !! Model A failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )

  if (!is.null(model_full)) {
    fc_full   <- forecast(model_full, xreg = xreg_test_full, h = n_test)
    pred_full <- as.numeric(fc_full$mean)

    all_metrics[[paste(comm, "full")]] <- compile_metrics(
      test_df$target_price, pred_full, train_df$target_price, model_full,
      paste0(comm, " — Full xreg")
    )
    all_diag[[paste(comm, "full")]]  <- residual_diagnostics(model_full, paste0(comm, " — Full xreg"))
    coefs_full                        <- extract_coefs(model_full, paste0(comm, " — Full xreg"))
    all_coefs[[paste(comm, "full")]] <- coefs_full

    fc_list[["Full xreg"]] <- fc_full

    cat("\n  Full xreg coefficient table:\n")
    print(coefs_full %>% select(term, estimate, std_err, t_stat, p_value, significant), n = Inf)

    plot_residuals(model_full, paste0(comm, " — Full xreg"))
    plot_coefficients(coefs_full, paste0(comm, " — Full xreg"))
  }

  # ── Model B: Dummies only ─────────────────
  if (length(avail_dummies) > 0) {
    xreg_train_dum <- build_xreg(train_df, avail_dummies)
    xreg_test_dum  <- build_xreg(test_df,  avail_dummies)

    cat("\n  Fitting Model B: Dummies only (", ncol(xreg_train_dum), "regressors)...\n")
    model_dum <- tryCatch(
      Arima(train_ts, order = pdq,
            seasonal = list(order = PDQ, period = FREQ),
            xreg = xreg_train_dum, include.drift = FALSE),
      error = function(e) { cat("  !! Model B failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
    )

    if (!is.null(model_dum)) {
      fc_dum   <- forecast(model_dum, xreg = xreg_test_dum, h = n_test)
      pred_dum <- as.numeric(fc_dum$mean)

      all_metrics[[paste(comm, "dummies")]] <- compile_metrics(
        test_df$target_price, pred_dum, train_df$target_price, model_dum,
        paste0(comm, " — Dummies only")
      )
      all_diag[[paste(comm, "dummies")]]  <- residual_diagnostics(model_dum, paste0(comm, " — Dummies only"))
      coefs_dum                            <- extract_coefs(model_dum, paste0(comm, " — Dummies only"))
      all_coefs[[paste(comm, "dummies")]] <- coefs_dum

      fc_list[["Dummies only"]] <- fc_dum

      cat("\n  Dummies-only coefficient table:\n")
      print(coefs_dum %>% select(term, estimate, std_err, t_stat, p_value, significant), n = Inf)

      plot_residuals(model_dum, paste0(comm, " — Dummies only"))
      plot_coefficients(coefs_dum, paste0(comm, " — Dummies only"))
    }
  }

  # ── Combined forecast plot ─────────────────
  if (length(fc_list) > 0) {
    plot_forecast(
      train_dates = train_df$date,  train_vals = train_df$target_price,
      test_dates  = test_df$date,   test_vals  = test_df$target_price,
      fc_list     = fc_list,        label      = comm
    )
  }
}
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##   Commodity: Beetroot 
## ============================================================ 
##   Train: 2016-01-22 -> 2024-12-20 (446 weeks)
##   Test:  2025-01-20 -> 2026-02-16 (28 weeks)
##   Loaded ARIMA order: ARIMA(1,1,4)(0,0,0)[52]
##   xreg vars: api_energy_and_lubricants, api_fertilisers_and_soil_improvers, api_plant_protection_products, api_fresh_fruit, api_fresh_vegetables, fuel_petrol_price, fuel_diesel_price, sppi_road_freight 
##   dummies:   shock_2021q4_2023q1, post_shock 
## 
##   Fitting Model A: Full xreg ( 10 regressors)...
## Warning in forecast.forecast_ARIMA(model_full, xreg = xreg_test_full, h =
## n_test): Upper prediction intervals are not finite.
## 
##   Full xreg coefficient table:
## # A tibble: 15 × 6
##    term                            estimate std_err   t_stat p_value significant
##    <chr>                              <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> <lgl>      
##  1 ar1                              8.23e-1 6.21e-2  13.2     0      TRUE       
##  2 ma1                             -1.01e+0 7.68e-2 -13.2     0      TRUE       
##  3 ma2                              1.95e-1 6.97e-2   2.80    0.0052 TRUE       
##  4 ma3                             -2.49e-2 6.46e-2  -0.386   0.699  FALSE      
##  5 ma4                             -9.64e-2 4.69e-2  -2.06    0.0398 TRUE       
##  6 api_energy_and_lubricants       -1.34e-4 8.03e-4  -0.167   0.867  FALSE      
##  7 api_fertilisers_and_soil_impro…  5.37e-4 2.59e-4   2.08    0.0379 TRUE       
##  8 api_plant_protection_products   -1.83e-3 1.29e-3  -1.42    0.157  FALSE      
##  9 api_fresh_fruit                 -3.45e-4 3.99e-4  -0.864   0.388  FALSE      
## 10 api_fresh_vegetables             6.77e-4 8.12e-4   0.833   0.405  FALSE      
## 11 fuel_petrol_price               -2.33e-3 1.52e-3  -1.53    0.125  FALSE      
## 12 fuel_diesel_price                1.13e-3 1.05e-3   1.07    0.282  FALSE      
## 13 sppi_road_freight               -1.41e-3 1.48e-3  -0.958   0.338  FALSE      
## 14 shock_2021q4_2023q1             -4.94e-2 3.11e-2  -1.59    0.112  FALSE      
## 15 post_shock                       1.17e-3 4.25e-2   0.0277  0.978  FALSE

## Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
## `height` was translated to `width`.

## 
##   Fitting Model B: Dummies only ( 2 regressors)...
## 
##   Dummies-only coefficient table:
## # A tibble: 7 × 6
##   term                estimate std_err  t_stat p_value significant
##   <chr>                  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <lgl>      
## 1 ar1                  0.861    0.0438  19.7    0      TRUE       
## 2 ma1                 -1.04     0.0630 -16.5    0      TRUE       
## 3 ma2                  0.205    0.0679   3.01   0.0026 TRUE       
## 4 ma3                 -0.0397   0.0640  -0.621  0.535  FALSE      
## 5 ma4                 -0.0857   0.0443  -1.93   0.0534 FALSE      
## 6 shock_2021q4_2023q1 -0.00813  0.0291  -0.279  0.78   FALSE      
## 7 post_shock           0.0398   0.0422   0.945  0.345  FALSE

## `height` was translated to `width`.

## Warning: Removed 28 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_ribbon()`).
## Warning: Removed 28 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_line()`).

metrics_df <- bind_rows(all_metrics)
diag_df    <- bind_rows(all_diag)
coefs_df   <- bind_rows(all_coefs)

arima_for_compare    <- arima_baseline %>% mutate(source = "arima_model.R (pre-run)")
all_metrics_combined <- bind_rows(
  metrics_df %>% mutate(source = "lm_arima_model.Rmd (this file)"),
  arima_for_compare
)

cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  LM-ARIMA MODEL PERFORMANCE (this file)\n")
##   LM-ARIMA MODEL PERFORMANCE (this file)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(metrics_df, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 2 × 8
##   model                      RMSE      MAE   MASE  sMAPE   MAPE    AIC    BIC
##   <chr>                     <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 Beetroot — Full xreg    NaN     NaN      NaN    NaN    NaN    -1858. -1793.
## 2 Beetroot — Dummies only   0.073   0.0512   2.71   6.95   6.54 -1852. -1819.
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS\n")
##   RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(diag_df, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 2 × 9
##   model   resid_mean resid_sd ljung_box_lag10_p ljung_box_lag20_p normality_test
##   <chr>        <dbl>    <dbl>             <dbl>             <dbl> <chr>         
## 1 Beetro…    0.00266   0.0288             0.539             0.815 Shapiro-Wilk  
## 2 Beetro…    0.00237   0.0296             0.163             0.392 Shapiro-Wilk  
## # ℹ 3 more variables: normality_p <dbl>, autocorr_ok <lgl>, normality_ok <lgl>
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  FULL COMPARISON: LM-ARIMA vs ARIMA BASELINES\n")
##   FULL COMPARISON: LM-ARIMA vs ARIMA BASELINES
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(
  all_metrics_combined %>%
    select(model, RMSE, MAE, MASE, sMAPE, MAPE, AIC, BIC, source) %>%
    arrange(model),
  n = Inf
)
## # A tibble: 5 × 9
##   model                  RMSE      MAE   MASE  sMAPE   MAPE    AIC    BIC source
##   <chr>                 <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> <chr> 
## 1 Beetroot — ARIMA(…   0.0804   0.0601   3.18   8.26   7.74 -1845. -1833. arima…
## 2 Beetroot — Dummie…   0.073    0.0512   2.71   6.95   6.54 -1852. -1819. lm_ar…
## 3 Beetroot — Full x… NaN      NaN      NaN    NaN    NaN    -1858. -1793. lm_ar…
## 4 Beetroot — SARIMA…   0.0737   0.0505   2.68   6.85   6.48 -1514. -1494. arima…
## 5 Beetroot — auto.a…   0.0744   0.0529   2.80   7.20   6.77 -1853. -1829. arima…
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  BEST MODEL PER COMMODITY (by RMSE, across all models)\n")
##   BEST MODEL PER COMMODITY (by RMSE, across all models)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
all_metrics_combined %>%
  mutate(commodity = str_extract(model, "^.+(?= —)")) %>%
  group_by(commodity) %>%
  slice_min(RMSE, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(commodity, model, RMSE, MAE, MASE, AIC, source) %>%
  print(n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 7
##   commodity model                    RMSE    MAE  MASE    AIC source            
##   <chr>     <chr>                   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>             
## 1 Beetroot  Beetroot — Dummies only 0.073 0.0512  2.71 -1852. lm_arima_model.Rm…
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  AIC IMPROVEMENT: Full xreg vs auto.arima baseline\n")
##   AIC IMPROVEMENT: Full xreg vs auto.arima baseline
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
aic_compare <- all_metrics_combined %>%
  mutate(
    commodity = str_extract(model, "^.+(?= —)"),
    variant   = case_when(
      str_detect(model, "Full xreg")  ~ "full_xreg",
      str_detect(model, "auto.arima") ~ "auto_arima_baseline",
      TRUE                            ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(variant)) %>%
  select(commodity, variant, AIC) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = variant, values_from = AIC) %>%
  mutate(
    aic_improvement = round(auto_arima_baseline - full_xreg, 2),
    xreg_helps      = aic_improvement > 0
  )
print(aic_compare, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   commodity full_xreg auto_arima_baseline aic_improvement xreg_helps
##   <chr>         <dbl>               <dbl>           <dbl> <lgl>     
## 1 Beetroot     -1858.              -1853.            4.86 TRUE
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  SIGNIFICANT REGRESSORS\n")
##   SIGNIFICANT REGRESSORS
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
coefs_df %>%
  filter(significant, !str_detect(term, "^ar|^ma|^sar|^sma|intercept|drift")) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std_err, p_value) %>%
  arrange(model, p_value) %>%
  print(n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   model                term                             estimate std_err p_value
##   <chr>                <chr>                               <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 Beetroot — Full xreg api_fertilisers_and_soil_improv… 0.000537 2.59e-4  0.0379
write_csv(metrics_df,           file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "lm_arima_metrics_filtered.csv"))
write_csv(diag_df,              file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "lm_arima_residual_diagnostics_filtered.csv"))
write_csv(coefs_df,             file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "lm_arima_coefficients_filtered.csv"))
write_csv(all_metrics_combined, file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "all_models_comparison_filtered.csv"))

cat("\n✅ Done.\n")
## 
## ✅ Done.
